subtasks.description: Scan and extract IAM policies from AWS IAM service, identifying all active role definitions, permission boundaries, and trust relationships
Query CloudTrail logs from SQL warehouse to identify API calls, user activities, timestamp sequences, and detect anomalous access patterns
Reconcile extracted IAM policies against CloudTrail audit log results and policy compliance registry to identify violations, unauthorized access attempts, and principle-of-least-privilege breaches
Synthesize violation findings, posture assessment, and remediation recommendations into a structured compliance report with severity levels and actionable guidance

plan.cost_rationale: Four-subtask plan balances scope and efficiency: (1) IAM extraction is mid-tier due to multiple policy types and trust relationships requiring careful field mapping; (2) CloudTrail query is mid-tier for standard SQL aggregation over activity logs; (3) Policy violation checking is frontier-tier because it requires reconciling two independent sources (IAM policies + CloudTrail logs) with inference against a policy registry to identify subtle breaches and posture classification; (4) Compliance report generation is mid-tier policy_action focused on governance decision documentation and remediation guidance. This plan avoids unnecessary subtasks by bundling all IAM scanning into one extract call and all CloudTrail querying into one sql_gen call, with cross_recon handling the critical violation detection logic upstream of the final compliance report.

output.error: Document extraction task cannot be completed
output.reason: No document provided in the task
output.details: The task describes a workflow for scanning AWS IAM policies and CloudTrail logs, but no actual document content, screenshot, or AWS console output was provided for extraction. To extract IAM policies, role definitions, permission boundaries, and trust relationships, please provide the actual IAM policy document, JSON configuration, or console output.

output.sql: SELECT log_id, user_id, permission, resource, access_time, action, result FROM access_log_facts ORDER BY access_time DESC LIMIT 1000

blocking_issues.details: Document extraction task cannot be completed without actual IAM policy JSON, AWS Console output, or CloudTrail export

blocking_issues.issue: SQL query defined but no result data provided
blocking_issues.details: CloudTrail audit log query exists but no actual log records returned for reconciliation

audit_status.reconciliation_impact: Cannot perform LEFT JOIN reconciliation without MongoDB (IAM policies) and SQL (CloudTrail logs) result sets
next_steps: Provide actual IAM policy document or AWS Console output
Provide CloudTrail audit log export or query results
Execute queries and return result sets for reconciliation
Re-run reconciliation task with populated data

output.reason: Task cannot be executed. Upstream audit tasks failed with INCOMPLETE status due to missing critical input data. The check_policy_violations task reports two blocking issues: (1) No IAM policy document provided - required for IAM scan; (2) CloudTrail logs lack actual result data - required for audit reconciliation. Compliance report synthesis requires valid violation findings and posture assessment data, which cannot be generated without these inputs.

blocking_issues.required_before_action: Provide IAM policy JSON document, AWS Console IAM policy screenshot, CloudTrail audit log export, Role trust relationship document, or Permission boundary policy definition
Provide CloudTrail audit log export or execute and return query result sets

output.remediation_path: 1) Supply required IAM policy and CloudTrail log data; 2) Re-execute audit_iam_scan and audit_cloudtrail_logs tasks; 3) Re-run reconciliation with populated result sets; 4) Re-submit compliance report synthesis task with valid upstream outputs
